The SATARC Club of Chaitanya Bharathi Institute of Technology (CBIT) organized a series
of cybersecurity-focused events in September 2024. These events aimed to engage students in
hands-on cybersecurity tasks while promoting knowledge sharing and practical application.
(3rd September 2024): An industry professional, Malla Reddy Donapati,
a Threat Hunter at Microsoft, delivered a talk providing insights into cybersecurity
(12th September 2024): A teaser event conducted near the canteen and H-
block to introduce students to AI prompt injection and cybersecurity techniques in
(13th September 2024): A competitive event where participants
applied AI-assisted password cracking and ethical hacking skills.
The primary goal of these events was to promote a deeper understanding of cybersecurity and
AI’s role in enhancing it. Each event focused on different aspects:
: Equip students with knowledge of real-world cybersecurity trends,
tools, and industry practices, encouraging them to pursue careers in the field.
: Generate excitement and introduce basic AI and cybersecurity concepts
to students in an informal, interactive setting.
: Provide hands-on experience with AI-assisted hacking, fostering
The Expert Talk was conducted in the CBIT assembly hall, with over 150 students in
, a Threat Hunter at Microsoft, provided a presentation
Best practices for defending against cyberattacks.
The growing role of AI in cybersecurity.
Career paths and opportunities in the cybersecurity industry.
This event was designed as a teaser to grab attention for the upcoming Hack The Bot
competition. The SATARC team set up stalls near the canteen and H-block to showcase a
small prototype of the Hack The Bot event. They introduced students to
and explained how it could be used to crack passwords. Participants were invited to engage
with the prototype, ask questions, and get a feel for what they would face in the competition.
The event successfully created a buzz among the student community, encouraging over 200
students to register for Hack The Bot.
This was the main event of the series. It consisted of two rounds, each challenging
participants to crack passwords using AI-generated clues. The competition took place in Lab
9 and Lab 10 in C-block. Participants were tasked with solving progressively harder
to receive clues based on carefully crafted prompts.
: Participants worked through 5 levels of password-cracking challenges. The
effectiveness of AI prompt generation and the time taken to solve each challenge
: The top 10 participants from each slot advanced to this round, where they
tackled 3 advanced password-cracking levels. The scoring system remained the same,
with Bounty Points awarded based on prompt quality and time.
The event attracted over 200 participants and concluded with a leaderboard showcasing the
top performers. Cash prizes and certificates were awarded to the winners.
Overview of hack the bot event
Increased Awareness of AI in Cybersecurity
Through the Buzz Event and Hack The Bot competition, participants gained firsthand
experience with AI tools and their application in password cracking and cybersecurity
problem-solving. This exposure encouraged students to explore AI-driven solutions
Participants developed practical skills in ethical hacking and password cracking
through the competition. The use of AI-assisted clues helped students enhance their
understanding of both AI and cybersecurity techniques.
One of the key takeaways from Hack The Bot was learning how to craft precise and
effective AI prompts to generate useful clues. Participants quickly adapted to this
challenge, improving their prompt-crafting skills in real-time, which is a valuable skill
The Expert Talk allowed students to interact with an experienced cybersecurity
professional, Malla Reddy Donapati, providing them with insights into the industry's
current trends, challenges, and career opportunities. This interaction broadened their
understanding of the professional landscape in cybersecurity.
Both the Buzz Event and Hack The Bot encouraged collaboration among participants.
Students worked together to solve challenges, fostering teamwork and improving their
The series of events saw significant participation, with over 200 students engaging
with the Buzz Event and Hack The Bot, and 150+ attending the Expert Talk. This high
level of engagement reflects the growing interest in cybersecurity and AI among the

Subhash Popuri is a versatile cybersecurity professional at EY India, specializing in both
Red Teaming and Blue Teaming. In his Red Team role, he assesses clients' infrastructure by
attacks through various stages like gaining access, escalating privileges, and
Subhash automates parts of this process and also evaluates physical security
at client locations. He assesses the effectiveness of the Blue Team's defenses and
findings in a clear and actionable manner.
